content: Stale Reflection Notes and the Cost of Unchecked Recall: Unchecked stale memory makes reflection look more reliable than the evidence supports.
ReflectCheck: Reflection Improves Reviews Only Under Evidence-Backed Verification: Reflection helps only when tied to evidence-backed verification and changed behavior.
MemoryPolicy: When Notes Help and When They Mislead: Memory must include retrieval and forgetting policy, not just storage.
Memory-Rich, Tool-Poor Research Assistants: Memory-rich tool-poor systems preserve continuity but struggle when they need fresh evidence outside stored notes.
Tool-Rich, Memoryless Review Systems: Tool-rich memoryless systems find fresh evidence but often lose earlier support during synthesis.
